title: DexJoCo: A Benchmark and Toolkit for Task-Oriented Dexterous Manipulation on MuJoCo
summary: DexJoCo is a benchmark and toolkit for task-oriented dexterous manipulation on MuJoCo, comprising 11 functionally grounded tasks that target tool-use, bimanual coordination, long-horizon execution, and reasoning — capabilities that distinguish dexterous hands from parallel grippers and that existing benchmarks underrepresent. It includes a low-cost data collection system and 1.1K collected trajectories across the tasks, with domain-randomization support for robustness evaluation. Benchmarking modern policies under visual and dynamics randomization, multi-task training, and action-head adaptation surfaces common limitations of current dexterous-manipulation methods and key challenges for future research.
